title: Calling and returning from a function

narration.essentials: We set up the call max(17, 42).
narration.detailed: The arguments are placed in the argument registers: the first, 17, goes into a0 (a1 gets 42 next). This is the calling convention the function will read from.

narration.essentials: `jal` jumps into the function and saves where to come back.
narration.detailed: `jal ra, max` calls the function: it jumps to `max` and saves the return address (the instruction after the call) into ra, so the function knows where to come back to.
narration.expert: jal writes PC + 4 into ra (x1) and sets the PC to PC + immediate; no source register is read.

narration.essentials: Inside the function, the arguments are compared.
narration.detailed: `bge a0, a1, done` compares the arguments. 17 is not >= 42, so the branch is not taken and the function falls through to copy a1 into a0 as the larger value.
narration.expert: The ALU evaluates the signed comparison a0 >= a1 -> 0 (not taken); the branch unit keeps PC + 4.

narration.essentials: The function returns 42, and the caller saves it.
narration.detailed: `ret` jumped back through ra to the caller, which now saves the returned result (42) into s0. The call is complete.
